NKJV.1: Now Saul was consenting to his death. At that time a great persecution arose against the church which was at Jerusalem; and they were all scattered throughout the regions of Judea and Samaria, except the apostles.
NKJV.2: And devout men carried Stephen to his burial, and made great lamentation over him.
NKJV.3: As for Saul, he made havoc of the church, entering every house, and dragging off men and women, committing them to prison.
NKJV.4: Therefore those who were scattered went everywhere preaching the word.
NKJV.5: Then Philip went down to the city of Samaria and preached Christ to them.
NKJV.6: And the multitudes with one accord heeded the things spoken by Philip, hearing and seeing the miracles which he did.
NKJV.7: For unclean spirits, crying with a loud voice, came out of many who were possessed; and many who were paralyzed and lame were healed.
NKJV.8: And there was great joy in that city.
NKJV.9: But there was a certain man called Simon, who previously practiced sorcery in the city and astonished the people of Samaria, claiming that he was someone great,
NKJV.10: to whom they all gave heed, from the least to the greatest, saying, “This man is the great power of God.”
NKJV.11: And they heeded him because he had astonished them with his sorceries for a long time.
NKJV.12: But when they believed Philip as he preached the things concerning the kingdom of God and the name of Jesus Christ, both men and women were baptized.
NKJV.13: Then Simon himself also believed; and when he was baptized he continued with Philip, and was amazed, seeing the miracles and signs which were done.
NKJV.14: Now when the apostles who were at Jerusalem heard that Samaria had received the word of God, they sent Peter and John to them,
NKJV.15: who, when they had come down, prayed for them that they might receive the Holy Spirit.
NKJV.16: For as yet He had fallen upon none of them. They had only been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
NKJV.17: Then they laid hands on them, and they received the Holy Spirit.
NKJV.18: And when Simon saw that through the laying on of the apostles’ hands the Holy Spirit was given, he offered them money,
NKJV.19: saying, “Give me this power also, that anyone on whom I lay hands may receive the Holy Spirit.”
NKJV.20: But Peter said to him, “Your money perish with you, because you thought that the gift of God could be purchased with money!
NKJV.21: You have neither part nor portion in this matter, for your heart is not right in the sight of God.
NKJV.22: Repent therefore of this your wickedness, and pray God if perhaps the thought of your heart may be forgiven you.
NKJV.23: For I see that you are poisoned by bitterness and bound by iniquity.”
NKJV.24: Then Simon answered and said, “Pray to the Lord for me, that none of the things which you have spoken may come upon me.”
NKJV.25: So when they had testified and preached the word of the Lord, they returned to Jerusalem, preaching the gospel in many villages of the Samaritans.
NKJV.26: Now an angel of the Lord spoke to Philip, saying, “Arise and go toward the south along the road which goes down from Jerusalem to Gaza.” This is desert.
NKJV.27: So he arose and went. And behold, a man of Ethiopia, a eunuch of great authority under Candace the queen of the Ethiopians, who had charge of all her treasury, and had come to Jerusalem to worship,
NKJV.28: was returning. And sitting in his chariot, he was reading Isaiah the prophet.
NKJV.29: Then the Spirit said to Philip, “Go near and overtake this chariot.”
NKJV.30: So Philip ran to him, and heard him reading the prophet Isaiah, and said, “Do you understand what you are reading?”
NKJV.31: And he said, “How can I, unless someone guides me?” And he asked Philip to come up and sit with him.
NKJV.32: The place in the Scripture which he read was this: “He was led as a sheep to the slaughter; And as a lamb before its shearer is silent, So He opened not His mouth.
NKJV.33: In His humiliation His justice was taken away, And who will declare His generation? For His life is taken from the earth.”
NKJV.34: So the eunuch answered Philip and said, “I ask you, of whom does the prophet say this, of himself or of some other man?”
NKJV.35: Then Philip opened his mouth, and beginning at this Scripture, preached Jesus to him.
NKJV.36: Now as they went down the road, they came to some water. And the eunuch said, “See, here is water. What hinders me from being baptized?”
NKJV.37: Then Philip said, “If you believe with all your heart, you may.” And he answered and said, “I believe that Jesus Christ is the Son of God.”
NKJV.38: So he commanded the chariot to stand still. And both Philip and the eunuch went down into the water, and he baptized him.
NKJV.39: Now when they came up out of the water, the Spirit of the Lord caught Philip away, so that the eunuch saw him no more; and he went on his way rejoicing.
NKJV.40: But Philip was found at Azotus. And passing through, he preached in all the cities till he came to Caesarea.

ESV.1: And Saul approved of his execution. And there arose on that day a great persecution against the church in Jerusalem, and they were all scattered throughout the regions of Judea and Samaria, except the apostles.
ESV.2: Devout men buried Stephen and made great lamentation over him.
ESV.3: But Saul was ravaging the church, and entering house after house, he dragged off men and women and committed them to prison.
ESV.4: Now those who were scattered went about preaching the word.
ESV.5: Philip went down to the city  of Samaria and proclaimed to them the Christ.
ESV.6: And the crowds with one accord paid attention to what was being said by Philip, when they heard him and saw the signs that he did.
ESV.7: For unclean spirits, crying out with a loud voice, came out of many who had them, and many who were paralyzed or lame were healed.
ESV.8: So there was much joy in that city.
ESV.9: But there was a man named Simon, who had previously practiced magic in the city and amazed the people of Samaria, saying that he himself was somebody great.
ESV.10: They all paid attention to him, from the least to the greatest, saying, “This man is the power of God that is called Great.”
ESV.11: And they paid attention to him because for a long time he had amazed them with his magic.
ESV.12: But when they believed Philip as he preached good news about the kingdom of God and the name of Jesus Christ, they were baptized, both men and women.
ESV.13: Even Simon himself believed, and after being baptized he continued with Philip. And seeing signs and great miracles  performed, he was amazed.
ESV.14: Now when the apostles at Jerusalem heard that Samaria had received the word of God, they sent to them Peter and John,
ESV.15: who came down and prayed for them that they might receive the Holy Spirit,
ESV.16: for he had not yet fallen on any of them, but they had only been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
ESV.17: Then they laid their hands on them and they received the Holy Spirit.
ESV.18: Now when Simon saw that the Spirit was given through the laying on of the apostles' hands, he offered them money,
ESV.19: saying, “Give me this power also, so that anyone on whom I lay my hands may receive the Holy Spirit.”
ESV.20: But Peter said to him, “May your silver perish with you, because you thought you could obtain the gift of God with money!
ESV.21: You have neither part nor lot in this matter, for your heart is not right before God.
ESV.22: Repent, therefore, of this wickedness of yours, and pray to the Lord that, if possible, the intent of your heart may be forgiven you.
ESV.23: For I see that you are in the gall  of bitterness and in the bond of iniquity.”
ESV.24: And Simon answered, “Pray for me to the Lord, that nothing of what you have said may come upon me.”
ESV.25: Now when they had testified and spoken the word of the Lord, they returned to Jerusalem, preaching the gospel to many villages of the Samaritans.
ESV.26: Now an angel of the Lord said to Philip, “Rise and go toward the south  to the road that goes down from Jerusalem to Gaza.” This is a desert place.
ESV.27: And he rose and went. And there was an Ethiopian, a eunuch, a court official of Candace, queen of the Ethiopians, who was in charge of all her treasure. He had come to Jerusalem to worship
ESV.28: and was returning, seated in his chariot, and he was reading the prophet Isaiah.
ESV.29: And the Spirit said to Philip, “Go over and join this chariot.”
ESV.30: So Philip ran to him and heard him reading Isaiah the prophet and asked, “Do you understand what you are reading?”
ESV.31: And he said, “How can I, unless someone guides me?” And he invited Philip to come up and sit with him.
ESV.32: Now the passage of the Scripture that he was reading was this: “Like a sheep he was led to the slaughter and like a lamb before its shearer is silent, so he opens not his mouth.
ESV.33: In his humiliation justice was denied him. Who can describe his generation? For his life is taken away from the earth.”
ESV.34: And the eunuch said to Philip, “About whom, I ask you, does the prophet say this, about himself or about someone else?”
ESV.35: Then Philip opened his mouth, and beginning with this Scripture he told him the good news about Jesus.
ESV.36: And as they were going along the road they came to some water, and the eunuch said, “See, here is water! What prevents me from being baptized?”
ESV.38: And he commanded the chariot to stop, and they both went down into the water, Philip and the eunuch, and he baptized him.
ESV.39: And when they came up out of the water, the Spirit of the Lord carried Philip away, and the eunuch saw him no more, and went on his way rejoicing.
ESV.40: But Philip found himself at Azotus, and as he passed through he preached the gospel to all the towns until he came to Caesarea.

NASB.1: Saul was in hearty agreement with putting him to death. And on that day a great persecution began against the church in Jerusalem, and they were all scattered throughout the regions of Judea and Samaria, except the apostles.
NASB.2: [Some] devout men buried Stephen, and made loud lamentation over him.
NASB.3: But Saul [began] ravaging the church, entering house after house, and dragging off men and women, he would put them in prison.
NASB.4: Therefore, those who had been scattered went about preaching the word.
NASB.5: Philip went down to the city of Samaria and [began] proclaiming Christ to them.
NASB.6: The crowds with one accord were giving attention to what was said by Philip, as they heard and saw the signs which he was performing.
NASB.7: For [in the case of] many who had unclean spirits, they were coming out [of them] shouting with a loud voice; and many who had been paralyzed and lame were healed.
NASB.8: So there was much rejoicing in that city.
NASB.9: Now there was a man named Simon, who formerly was practicing magic in the city and astonishing the people of Samaria, claiming to be someone great;
NASB.10: and they all, from smallest to greatest, were giving attention to him, saying, 'This man is what is called the Great Power of God.'
NASB.11: And they were giving him attention because he had for a long time astonished them with his magic arts.
NASB.12: But when they believed Philip preaching the good news about the kingdom of God and the name of Jesus Christ, they were being baptized, men and women alike.
NASB.13: Even Simon himself believed; and after being baptized, he continued on with Philip, and as he observed signs and great miracles taking place, he was constantly amazed.
NASB.14: Now when the apostles in Jerusalem heard that Samaria had received the word of God, they sent them Peter and John,
NASB.15: who came down and prayed for them that they might receive the Holy Spirit.
NASB.16: For He had not yet fallen upon any of them; they had simply been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
NASB.17: Then they [began] laying their hands on them, and they were receiving the Holy Spirit.
NASB.18: Now when Simon saw that the Spirit was bestowed through the laying on of the apostles' hands, he offered them money,
NASB.19: saying, 'Give this authority to me as well, so that everyone on whom I lay my hands may receive the Holy Spirit.'
NASB.20: But Peter said to him, 'May your silver perish with you, because you thought you could obtain the gift of God with money!
NASB.21: 'You have no part or portion in this matter, for your heart is not right before God.
NASB.22: 'Therefore repent of this wickedness of yours, and pray the Lord that, if possible, the intention of your heart may be forgiven you.
NASB.23: 'For I see that you are in the gall of bitterness and in the bondage of iniquity.'
NASB.24: But Simon answered and said, 'Pray to the Lord for me yourselves, so that nothing of what you have said may come upon me.'
NASB.25: So, when they had solemnly testified and spoken the word of the Lord, they started back to Jerusalem, and were preaching the gospel to many villages of the Samaritans.
NASB.26: But an angel of the Lord spoke to Philip saying, 'Get up and go south to the road that descends from Jerusalem to Gaza.' (This is a desert [road].)
NASB.27: So he got up and went; and there was an Ethiopian eunuch, a court official of Candace, queen of the Ethiopians, who was in charge of all her treasure; and he had come to Jerusalem to worship,
NASB.28: and he was returning and sitting in his chariot, and was reading the prophet Isaiah.
NASB.29: Then the Spirit said to Philip, 'Go up and join this chariot.'
NASB.30: Philip ran up and heard him reading Isaiah the prophet, and said, 'Do you understand what you are reading?'
NASB.31: And he said, 'Well, how could I, unless someone guides me?' And he invited Philip to come up and sit with him.
NASB.32: Now the passage of Scripture which he was reading was this: 'HE WAS LED AS A SHEEP TO SLAUGHTER; AND AS A LAMB BEFORE ITS SHEARER IS SILENT, SO HE DOES NOT OPEN HIS MOUTH.
NASB.33: 'IN HUMILIATION HIS JUDGMENT WAS TAKEN AWAY; WHO WILL RELATE HIS GENERATION? FOR HIS LIFE IS REMOVED FROM THE EARTH.'
NASB.34: The eunuch answered Philip and said, 'Please [tell me], of whom does the prophet say this? Of himself or of someone else?'
NASB.35: Then Philip opened his mouth, and beginning from this Scripture he preached Jesus to him.
NASB.36: As they went along the road they came to some water; and the eunuch said, 'Look! Water! What prevents me from being baptized?'
NASB.37: [And Philip said, 'If you believe with all your heart, you may.' And he answered and said, 'I believe that Jesus Christ is the Son of God.]'
NASB.38: And he ordered the chariot to stop; and they both went down into the water, Philip as well as the eunuch, and he baptized him.
NASB.39: When they came up out of the water, the Spirit of the Lord snatched Philip away; and the eunuch no longer saw him, but went on his way rejoicing.
NASB.40: But Philip found himself at Azotus, and as he passed through he kept preaching the gospel to all the cities until he came to Caesarea.

NLT.1: Saul was one of the witnesses, and he agreed completely with the killing of Stephen. Persecution Scatters the Believers A great wave of persecution began that day, sweeping over the church in Jerusalem; and all the believers except the apostles were scattered through the regions of Judea and Samaria.
NLT.2: (Some devout men came and buried Stephen with great mourning.)
NLT.3: But Saul was going everywhere to destroy the church. He went from house to house, dragging out both men and women to throw them into prison.
NLT.4: But the believers who were scattered preached the Good News about Jesus wherever they went.
NLT.5: Philip, for example, went to the city of Samaria and told the people there about the Messiah.
NLT.6: Crowds listened intently to Philip because they were eager to hear his message and see the miraculous signs he did.
NLT.7: Many evil spirits were cast out, screaming as they left their victims. And many who had been paralyzed or lame were healed.
NLT.8: So there was great joy in that city.
NLT.9: A man named Simon had been a sorcerer there for many years, amazing the people of Samaria and claiming to be someone great.
NLT.10: Everyone, from the least to the greatest, often spoke of him as “the Great One — the Power of God.”
NLT.11: They listened closely to him because for a long time he had astounded them with his magic.
NLT.12: But now the people believed Philip’s message of Good News concerning the Kingdom of God and the name of Jesus Christ. As a result, many men and women were baptized.
NLT.13: Then Simon himself believed and was baptized. He began following Philip wherever he went, and he was amazed by the signs and great miracles Philip performed.
NLT.14: When the apostles in Jerusalem heard that the people of Samaria had accepted God’s message, they sent Peter and John there.
NLT.15: As soon as they arrived, they prayed for these new believers to receive the Holy Spirit.
NLT.16: The Holy Spirit had not yet come upon any of them, for they had only been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
NLT.17: Then Peter and John laid their hands upon these believers, and they received the Holy Spirit.
NLT.18: When Simon saw that the Spirit was given when the apostles laid their hands on people, he offered them money to buy this power.
NLT.19: “Let me have this power, too,” he exclaimed, “so that when I lay my hands on people, they will receive the Holy Spirit!”
NLT.20: But Peter replied, “May your money be destroyed with you for thinking God’s gift can be bought!
NLT.21: You can have no part in this, for your heart is not right with God.
NLT.22: Repent of your wickedness and pray to the Lord. Perhaps he will forgive your evil thoughts,
NLT.23: for I can see that you are full of bitter jealousy and are held captive by sin.”
NLT.24: “Pray to the Lord for me,” Simon exclaimed, “that these terrible things you’ve said won’t happen to me!”
NLT.25: After testifying and preaching the word of the Lord in Samaria, Peter and John returned to Jerusalem. And they stopped in many Samaritan villages along the way to preach the Good News.
NLT.26: As for Philip, an angel of the Lord said to him, “Go south down the desert road that runs from Jerusalem to Gaza.”
NLT.27: So he started out, and he met the treasurer of Ethiopia, a eunuch of great authority under the Kandake, the queen of Ethiopia. The eunuch had gone to Jerusalem to worship,
NLT.28: and he was now returning. Seated in his carriage, he was reading aloud from the book of the prophet Isaiah.
NLT.29: The Holy Spirit said to Philip, “Go over and walk along beside the carriage.”
NLT.30: Philip ran over and heard the man reading from the prophet Isaiah. Philip asked, “Do you understand what you are reading?”
NLT.31: The man replied, “How can I, unless someone instructs me?” And he urged Philip to come up into the carriage and sit with him.
NLT.32:
The passage of Scripture he had been reading was this: “He was led like a sheep to the slaughter.
And as a lamb is silent before the shearers,
he did not open his mouth.
NLT.33:
He was humiliated and received no justice.
Who can speak of his descendants?
For his life was taken from the earth.”
NLT.34: The eunuch asked Philip, “Tell me, was the prophet talking about himself or someone else?”
NLT.35: So beginning with this same Scripture, Philip told him the Good News about Jesus.
NLT.36: As they rode along, they came to some water, and the eunuch said, “Look! There’s some water! Why can’t I be baptized?”
NLT.38: He ordered the carriage to stop, and they went down into the water, and Philip baptized him.
NLT.39: When they came up out of the water, the Spirit of the Lord snatched Philip away. The eunuch never saw him again but went on his way rejoicing.
NLT.40: Meanwhile, Philip found himself farther north at the town of Azotus. He preached the Good News there and in every town along the way until he came to Caesarea.

WEB.1: Saul was consenting to his death. A great persecution arose against the assembly which was in Jerusalem in that day. They were all scattered abroad throughout the regions of Judea and Samaria, except for the apostles.
WEB.2: Devout men buried Stephen, and lamented greatly over him.
WEB.3: But Saul ravaged the assembly, entering into every house, and dragged both men and women off to prison.
WEB.4: Therefore those who were scattered abroad went around preaching the word.
WEB.5: Philip went down to the city of Samaria, and proclaimed to them the Christ.
WEB.6: The multitudes listened with one accord to the things that were spoken by Philip, when they heard and saw the signs which he did.
WEB.7: For unclean spirits came out of many of those who had them. They came out, crying with a loud voice. Many who had been paralyzed and lame were healed.
WEB.8: There was great joy in that city.
WEB.9: But there was a certain man, Simon by name, who used to practice sorcery in the city, and amazed the people of Samaria, making himself out to be some great one,
WEB.10: to whom they all listened, from the least to the greatest, saying, “This man is that great power of God.”
WEB.11: They listened to him, because for a long time he had amazed them with his sorceries.
WEB.12: But when they believed Philip preaching good news concerning God’s Kingdom and the name of Jesus Christ, they were baptized, both men and women.
WEB.13: Simon himself also believed. Being baptized, he continued with Philip. Seeing signs and great miracles occurring, he was amazed.
WEB.14: Now when the apostles who were at Jerusalem heard that Samaria had received the word of God, they sent Peter and John to them,
WEB.15: who, when they had come down, prayed for them, that they might receive the Holy Spirit;
WEB.16: for as yet he had fallen on none of them. They had only been baptized in the name of Christ Jesus.
WEB.17: Then they laid their hands on them, and they received the Holy Spirit.
WEB.18: Now when Simon saw that the Holy Spirit was given through the laying on of the apostles’ hands, he offered them money,
WEB.19: saying, “Give me also this power, that whomever I lay my hands on may receive the Holy Spirit.”
WEB.20: But Peter said to him, “May your silver perish with you, because you thought you could obtain the gift of God with money!
WEB.21: You have neither part nor lot in this matter, for your heart isn’t right before God.
WEB.22: Repent therefore of this, your wickedness, and ask God if perhaps the thought of your heart may be forgiven you.
WEB.23: For I see that you are in the poison of bitterness and in the bondage of iniquity.”
WEB.24: Simon answered, “Pray for me to the Lord, that none of the things which you have spoken happen to me.”
WEB.25: They therefore, when they had testified and spoken the word of the Lord, returned to Jerusalem, and preached the Good News to many villages of the Samaritans.
WEB.26: But an angel of the Lord spoke to Philip, saying, “Arise, and go toward the south to the way that goes down from Jerusalem to Gaza. This is a desert.”
WEB.27: He arose and went; and behold, there was a man of Ethiopia, a eunuch of great authority under Candace, queen of the Ethiopians, who was over all her treasure, who had come to Jerusalem to worship.
WEB.28: He was returning and sitting in his chariot, and was reading the prophet Isaiah.
WEB.29: The Spirit said to Philip, “Go near, and join yourself to this chariot.”
WEB.30: Philip ran to him, and heard him reading Isaiah the prophet, and said, “Do you understand what you are reading?”
WEB.31: He said, “How can I, unless someone explains it to me?” He begged Philip to come up and sit with him.
WEB.32: Now the passage of the Scripture which he was reading was this, “He was led as a sheep to the slaughter. As a lamb before his shearer is silent, so he doesn’t open his mouth.
WEB.33: In his humiliation, his judgment was taken away. Who will declare His generation? For his life is taken from the earth.”
WEB.34: The eunuch answered Philip, “Who is the prophet talking about? About himself, or about someone else?”
WEB.35: Philip opened his mouth, and beginning from this Scripture, preached to him about Jesus.
WEB.36: As they went on the way, they came to some water, and the eunuch said, “Behold, here is water. What is keeping me from being baptized?”
WEB.38: He commanded the chariot to stand still, and they both went down into the water, both Philip and the eunuch, and he baptized him.
WEB.39: When they came up out of the water, the Spirit of the Lord caught Philip away, and the eunuch didn’t see him any more, for he went on his way rejoicing.
WEB.40: But Philip was found at Azotus. Passing through, he preached the Good News to all the cities, until he came to Caesarea.

CSB17.1: Saul agreed with putting him to death. On that day a severe persecution broke out against the church in Jerusalem, and all except the apostles were scattered throughout the land of Judea and Samaria. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.2: Devout men buried Stephen and mourned deeply over him.
CSB17.3: Saul, <sup>ⓚ</sup> however, was ravaging the church. He would enter house after house, drag off men and women, and put them in prison. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.4: So those who were scattered went on their way preaching the word.
CSB17.5: Philip went down to a <sup>[1]</sup> city in Samaria and proclaimed the Messiah to them. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.6: The crowds were all paying attention to what Philip said, as they listened and saw the signs he was performing.
CSB17.7: For unclean spirits, crying out with a loud voice, came out of many who were possessed, and many who were paralyzed and lame were healed. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.8: So there was great joy in that city.
CSB17.9: A man named Simon had previously practiced sorcery in that city and amazed the Samaritan people, while claiming to be somebody great. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.10: They all paid attention to him, from the least of them to the greatest, and they said, “This man is called the Great Power of God.” <sup>[2]</sup> <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.11: They were attentive to him because he had amazed them with his sorceries for a long time.
CSB17.12: But when they believed Philip, as he proclaimed the good news about the kingdom of God and the name of Jesus Christ, both men and women were baptized. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.13: Even Simon himself believed. And after he was baptized, he followed Philip everywhere and was amazed as he observed the signs and great miracles <sup>ⓓ</sup> that were being performed.
CSB17.14: When the apostles who were at Jerusalem heard that Samaria had received the word of God, they sent Peter and John to them. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.15: After they went down there, they prayed for them so the Samaritans might receive the Holy Spirit because he had not yet come down on any of them.
CSB17.16: (They had only been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus. <sup>ⓕ</sup>)
CSB17.17: Then Peter and John laid their hands on them, and they received the Holy Spirit.
CSB17.18: When Simon saw that the Spirit <sup>[3]</sup> was given through the laying on of the apostles’ hands, he offered them money,
CSB17.19: saying, “Give me this power also so that anyone I lay hands on may receive the Holy Spirit.”
CSB17.20: But Peter told him, “May your silver be destroyed with you, because you thought you could obtain the gift of God with money! <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.21: You have no part or share in this matter, because your heart is not right before God. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.22: Therefore repent of this wickedness of yours, and pray to the Lord that, if possible, your heart’s intent may be forgiven.
CSB17.23: For I see you are poisoned by bitterness and bound by wickedness.” <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.24: “Pray to the Lord for me,” Simon replied, “so that nothing you have said may happen to me.” <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.25: So, after they had testified and spoken the word of the Lord, they traveled back to Jerusalem, preaching the gospel in many villages of the Samaritans.
CSB17.26: An angel of the Lord spoke to Philip: “Get up and go south to the road that goes down from Jerusalem to Gaza.” (This is the desert road. <sup>[4]</sup> <sup>ⓚ</sup>)
CSB17.27: So he got up and went. There was an Ethiopian man, a eunuch <sup>ⓛ</sup> and high official of Candace, queen of the Ethiopians, who was in charge of her entire treasury. He had come to worship in Jerusalem <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.28: and was sitting in his chariot on his way home, reading the prophet Isaiah aloud.
CSB17.29: The Spirit told Philip, “Go and join that chariot.” <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.30: When Philip ran up to it, he heard him reading the prophet Isaiah, and said, “Do you understand what you’re reading?”

CSB17.32:
Now the Scripture passage he was reading was this: He was led like a sheep to the slaughter,
and as a lamb is silent before its shearer,
so he does not open his mouth.
CSB17.33:
In his humiliation justice was denied him.
Who will describe his generation?
For his life is taken from the earth. [5] ⓐ
CSB17.34: The eunuch said to Philip, “I ask you, who is the prophet saying this about — himself or someone else?”
CSB17.35: Philip proceeded to tell him the good news about Jesus, beginning with that Scripture. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.36: As they were traveling down the road, they came to some water. The eunuch said, “Look, there’s water. What would keep me from being baptized?” <sup>[6]</sup>
CSB17.38: So he ordered the chariot to stop, and both Philip and the eunuch went down into the water, and he baptized him.
CSB17.39: When they came up out of the water, the Spirit of the Lord <sup>ⓒ</sup> carried Philip away, and the eunuch did not see him any longer but went on his way rejoicing.
CSB17.40: Philip appeared in <sup>[7]</sup> Azotus, <sup>[8]</sup> and he was traveling and preaching the gospel in all the towns until he came to Caesarea. <sup>ⓓ</sup>

AMP.1:
Saul wholeheartedly approved of Stephen's death.
And on that day a great and relentless persecution broke out against the church in Jerusalem; and the believers were all scattered throughout the regions of Judea and Samaria, except for the apostles.
AMP.2: Some devout men buried Stephen, and mourned greatly over him [expressing a personal sense of loss].
AMP.3: But Saul began ravaging the church [and assaulting believers]; entering house after house and dragging off men and women, putting them in prison.
AMP.4: Now those [believers] who had been scattered went from place to place preaching the word [the good news of salvation through Christ].
AMP.5: Philip [the evangelist] went down to the city of Samaria and began proclaiming Christ (the Messiah, the Anointed) to them.
AMP.6: The crowds gathered and were paying close attention to everything Philip said, as they heard [the message] and saw the [miraculous] signs which he was doing [validating his message].
AMP.7: For unclean spirits (demons), shouting loudly, were coming out of many who were possessed; and many who had been paralyzed and lame were healed.
AMP.8: So there was great rejoicing in that city.
AMP.9: Now there was a man named Simon, who previously practiced magic in the city and amazed the people of Samaria, claiming to be someone great.
AMP.10: They all paid [a great deal of] attention to him, from the least to the greatest, saying, "This man is what is called the Great Power of God!"
AMP.11: They were paying attention to him because for a long time he had mystified and dazzled them with his magic.
AMP.12: But when they believed Philip as he preached the good news about the kingdom of God and the name of Jesus Christ, they were being baptized, both men and women.
AMP.13: Even Simon believed [Philip's message of salvation]; and after being baptized, he continued on with Philip, and as he watched the attesting signs and great miracles taking place, he was constantly amazed.
AMP.14: When the apostles in Jerusalem heard that [the people of] Samaria had accepted the word of God, they sent Peter and John to them.
AMP.15: They came down and prayed for them that they might receive the Holy Spirit;
AMP.16: for He had not yet fallen on any of them; they had simply been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus [as His possession].
AMP.17: Then Peter and John laid their hands on them [one by one], and they received the Holy Spirit.
AMP.18: Now when Simon saw that the Spirit was given through the laying on of the apostles' hands, he offered them money,
AMP.19: saying, "Give me this authority and power too, so that anyone on whom I lay my hands may receive the Holy Spirit."
AMP.20: But Peter said to him, "May your money be destroyed along with you, because you thought you could buy the [free] gift of God with money!
AMP.21: You have no part or share in this matter, because your heart (motive, purpose) is not right before God.
AMP.22: So repent of this wickedness of yours, and pray to the Lord that, if possible, this thought of your heart may be forgiven you.
AMP.23: For I see that you are provoked by bitterness and bound by sin."
AMP.24: But Simon answered, "Pray to the Lord for me both of you, so that nothing of what you have said will come upon me."
AMP.25: So, when Peter and John had given their testimony and preached the word of the Lord, they started back to Jerusalem, preaching the good news [about salvation] in many Samaritan villages [along the way].
AMP.26: But an angel of the Lord said to Philip, "Get up and go south to the road that runs from Jerusalem down to Gaza." (This is a desert road).
AMP.27: So he got up and went; and there was an Ethiopian eunuch [a man of great authority], a court official of Candace, queen of the Ethiopians, who was in charge of all her treasure. He had come to Jerusalem to worship,
AMP.28: and he was returning, and sitting in his chariot he was reading [the scroll of] the prophet Isaiah.
AMP.29: Then the [Holy] Spirit said to Philip, "Go up and join this chariot."
AMP.30: Philip ran up and heard the man reading the prophet Isaiah, and asked, "Do you understand what you are reading?"
AMP.31: And he said, "Well, how could I [understand] unless someone guides me [correctly]?" And he invited Philip to come up and sit with him.
AMP.32:
Now this was the passage of Scripture which he was reading:
"Like a sheep He was led to the slaughter;
And as a lamb before its shearer is silent,
So He does not open His mouth.
AMP.33:
"In humiliation His judgment was taken away [justice was denied Him].
Who will describe His generation?
For His life is taken from the earth."
AMP.34: The eunuch replied to Philip, "Please tell me, about whom does the prophet say this? About himself or about someone else?"
AMP.35: Then Philip spoke and beginning with this Scripture he preached Jesus to him [explaining that He is the promised Messiah and the source of salvation].
AMP.36: As they continued along the road, they came to some water; and the eunuch exclaimed, "Look! Water! What forbids me from being baptized?"
AMP.37: [Philip said to him, "If you believe with all your heart, you may." And he replied, "I do believe that Jesus Christ is the Son of God."]
AMP.38: And he ordered that the chariot be stopped; and both Philip and the eunuch went down into the water, and Philip baptized him.
AMP.39: When they came up out of the water, the Spirit of the Lord [suddenly] took Philip [and carried him] away [to a different place]; and the eunuch no longer saw him, but he went on his way rejoicing.
AMP.40: But Philip found himself at Azotus, and as he passed through he preached the good news [of salvation] to all the cities, until he came to Caesarea [Maritima].

MSG.1: That set off a terrific persecution of the church in Jerusalem. The believers were all scattered throughout Judea and Samaria. All, that is, but the apostles.
MSG.2: Good and brave men buried Stephen, giving him a solemn funeral—not many dry eyes that day!
MSG.3: And Saul just went wild, devastating the church, entering house after house after house, dragging men and women off to jail.
MSG.4: Forced to leave home base, the Christians all became missionaries. Wherever they were scattered, they preached the Message about Jesus.
MSG.5: Going down to a Samaritan city, Philip proclaimed the Message of the Messiah.
MSG.6: When the people heard what he had to say and saw the miracles, the clear signs of God's action, they hung on his every word.
MSG.7: Many who could neither stand nor walk were healed that day. The evil spirits protested loudly as they were sent on their way.
MSG.8: And what joy in the city!
MSG.9: Previous to Philip's arrival, a certain Simon had practiced magic in the city, posing as a famous man and dazzling all the Samaritans with his wizardry.
MSG.10: He had them all, from little children to old men, eating out of his hand. They all thought he had supernatural powers, and called him "the Great Wizard."
MSG.11: He had been around a long time and everyone was more or less in awe of him.
MSG.12: But when Philip came to town announcing the news of God's kingdom and proclaiming the name of Jesus Christ, they forgot Simon and were baptized, becoming believers right and left!
MSG.13: Even Simon himself believed and was baptized. From that moment he was like Philip's shadow, so fascinated with all the God-signs and miracles that he wouldn't leave Philip's side.
MSG.14: When the apostles in Jerusalem received the report that Samaria had accepted God's Message, they sent Peter and John down
MSG.15: to pray for them to receive the Holy Spirit.
MSG.16: Up to this point they had only been baptized in the name of the Master Jesus; the Holy Spirit hadn't yet fallen on them.
MSG.17: Then the apostles laid their hands on them and they did receive the Holy Spirit.
MSG.18: When Simon saw that the apostles by merely laying on hands conferred the Spirit, he pulled out his money, excited,
MSG.19: and said, "Sell me your secret! Show me how you did that! How much do you want? Name your price!"
MSG.20: Peter said, "To hell with your money! And you along with it. Why, that's unthinkable—trying to buy God's gift!
MSG.21: You'll never be part of what God is doing by striking bargains and offering bribes.
MSG.22: Change your ways—and now! Ask the Master to forgive you for trying to use God to make money.
MSG.23: I can see this is an old habit with you; you reek with money-lust."
MSG.24: "Oh!" said Simon, "pray for me! Pray to the Master that nothing like that will ever happen to me!"
MSG.25: And with that, the apostles were on their way, continuing to witness and spread the Message of God's salvation, preaching in every Samaritan town they passed through on their return to Jerusalem.
MSG.26: Later God's angel spoke to Philip: "At noon today I want you to walk over to that desolate road that goes from Jerusalem down to Gaza."
MSG.27: He got up and went. He met an Ethiopian eunuch coming down the road. The eunuch had been on a pilgrimage to Jerusalem and was returning to Ethiopia, where he was minister in charge of all the finances of Candace, queen of the Ethiopians.
MSG.28: He was riding in a chariot and reading the prophet Isaiah.
MSG.29: The Spirit told Philip, "Climb into the chariot."
MSG.30: Running up alongside, Philip heard the eunuch reading Isaiah and asked, "Do you understand what you're reading?"
MSG.31: He answered, "How can I without some help?" and invited Philip into the chariot with him.
MSG.32: The passage he was reading was this: As a sheep led to slaughter, and quiet as a lamb being sheared, He was silent, saying nothing.
MSG.33: He was mocked and put down, never got a fair trial. But who now can count his kin since he's been taken from the earth?
MSG.34: The eunuch said, "Tell me, who is the prophet talking about: himself or some other?"
MSG.35: Philip grabbed his chance. Using this passage as his text, he preached Jesus to him.
MSG.36: As they continued down the road, they came to a stream of water. The eunuch said, "Here's water. Why can't I be baptized?"
MSG.38: He ordered the chariot to stop. They both went down to the water, and Philip baptized him on the spot.
MSG.39: When they came up out of the water, the Spirit of God suddenly took Philip off, and that was the last the eunuch saw of him. But he didn't mind. He had what he'd come for and went on down the road as happy as he could be.
MSG.40: Philip showed up in Azotus and continued north, preaching the Message in all the villages along that route until he arrived at Caesarea.

NIV.1:
And Saul was there, giving approval to his death.
The Church Persecuted and Scattered
On that day a great persecution broke out against the church at Jerusalem, and all except the apostles were scattered throughout Judea and Samaria.
NIV.2: Godly men buried Stephen and mourned deeply for him.
NIV.3: But Saul began to destroy the church. Going from house to house, he dragged off men and women and put them in prison.
NIV.4:
Philip in Samaria
Those who had been scattered preached the word wherever they went.
NIV.5: Philip went down to a city in Samaria and proclaimed the Christ there.
NIV.6: When the crowds heard Philip and saw the miraculous signs he did, they all paid close attention to what he said.
NIV.7: With shrieks, evil spirits came out of many, and many paralytics and cripples were healed.
NIV.8: So there was great joy in that city.
NIV.9:
Simon the Sorcerer
Now for some time a man named Simon had practiced sorcery in the city and amazed all the people of Samaria. He boasted that he was someone great,
NIV.10: and all the people, both high and low, gave him their attention and exclaimed, “This man is the divine power known as the Great Power.”
NIV.11: They followed him because he had amazed them for a long time with his magic.
NIV.12: But when they believed Philip as he preached the good news of the kingdom of God and the name of Jesus Christ, they were baptized, both men and women.
NIV.13: Simon himself believed and was baptized. And he followed Philip everywhere, astonished by the great signs and miracles he saw.
NIV.14: When the apostles in Jerusalem heard that Samaria had accepted the word of God, they sent Peter and John to them.
NIV.15: When they arrived, they prayed for them that they might receive the Holy Spirit,
NIV.16: because the Holy Spirit had not yet come upon any of them; they had simply been baptized into the name of the Lord Jesus.
NIV.17: Then Peter and John placed their hands on them, and they received the Holy Spirit.
NIV.18: When Simon saw that the Spirit was given at the laying on of the apostles’ hands, he offered them money
NIV.19: and said, “Give me also this ability so that everyone on whom I lay my hands may receive the Holy Spirit.”
NIV.20: Peter answered: “May your money perish with you, because you thought you could buy the gift of God with money!
NIV.21: You have no part or share in this ministry, because your heart is not right before God.
NIV.22: Repent of this wickedness and pray to the Lord. Perhaps he will forgive you for having such a thought in your heart.
NIV.23: For I see that you are full of bitterness and captive to sin.”
NIV.24: Then Simon answered, “Pray to the Lord for me so that nothing you have said may happen to me.”
NIV.25: When they had testified and proclaimed the word of the Lord, Peter and John returned to Jerusalem, preaching the gospel in many Samaritan villages.
NIV.26:
Philip and the Ethiopian
Now an angel of the Lord said to Philip, “Go south to the road—the desert road—that goes down from Jerusalem to Gaza.”
NIV.27: So he started out, and on his way he met an Ethiopian eunuch, an important official in charge of all the treasury of Candace, queen of the Ethiopians. This man had gone to Jerusalem to worship,
NIV.28: and on his way home was sitting in his chariot reading the book of Isaiah the prophet.
NIV.29: The Spirit told Philip, “Go to that chariot and stay near it.”
NIV.30: Then Philip ran up to the chariot and heard the man reading Isaiah the prophet. “Do you understand what you are reading?” Philip asked.
NIV.31: “How can I,” he said, “unless someone explains it to me?” So he invited Philip to come up and sit with him.
NIV.32:
The eunuch was reading this passage of Scripture:
“He was led like a sheep to the slaughter,
and as a lamb before the shearer is silent,
so he did not open his mouth.
NIV.33:
In his humiliation he was deprived of justice.
Who can speak of his descendants?
For his life was taken from the earth.”
NIV.34: The eunuch asked Philip, “Tell me, please, who is the prophet talking about, himself or someone else?”
NIV.35: Then Philip began with that very passage of Scripture and told him the good news about Jesus.
NIV.36: As they traveled along the road, they came to some water and the eunuch said, “Look, here is water. Why shouldn’t I be baptized?”
NIV.38: And he gave orders to stop the chariot. Then both Philip and the eunuch went down into the water and Philip baptized him.
NIV.39: When they came up out of the water, the Spirit of the Lord suddenly took Philip away, and the eunuch did not see him again, but went on his way rejoicing.
NIV.40: Philip, however, appeared at Azotus and traveled about, preaching the gospel in all the towns until he reached Caesarea.

NRSVCE.1: And Saul approved of their killing him. Saul Persecutes the Church That day a severe persecution began against the church in Jerusalem, and all except the apostles were scattered throughout the countryside of Judea and Samaria.
NRSVCE.2: Devout men buried Stephen and made loud lamentation over him.
NRSVCE.3: But Saul was ravaging the church by entering house after house; dragging off both men and women, he committed them to prison.
NRSVCE.4: Now those who were scattered went from place to place, proclaiming the word.
NRSVCE.5: Philip went down to the city of Samaria and proclaimed the Messiah to them.
NRSVCE.6: The crowds with one accord listened eagerly to what was said by Philip, hearing and seeing the signs that he did,
NRSVCE.7: for unclean spirits, crying with loud shrieks, came out of many who were possessed; and many others who were paralyzed or lame were cured.
NRSVCE.8: So there was great joy in that city.
NRSVCE.9: Now a certain man named Simon had previously practiced magic in the city and amazed the people of Samaria, saying that he was someone great.
NRSVCE.10: All of them, from the least to the greatest, listened to him eagerly, saying, “This man is the power of God that is called Great.”
NRSVCE.11: And they listened eagerly to him because for a long time he had amazed them with his magic.
NRSVCE.12: But when they believed Philip, who was proclaiming the good news about the kingdom of God and the name of Jesus Christ, they were baptized, both men and women.
NRSVCE.13: Even Simon himself believed. After being baptized, he stayed constantly with Philip and was amazed when he saw the signs and great miracles that took place.
NRSVCE.14: Now when the apostles at Jerusalem heard that Samaria had accepted the word of God, they sent Peter and John to them.
NRSVCE.15: The two went down and prayed for them that they might receive the Holy Spirit
NRSVCE.16: (for as yet the Spirit had not come upon any of them; they had only been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus).
NRSVCE.17: Then Peter and John laid their hands on them, and they received the Holy Spirit.
NRSVCE.18: Now when Simon saw that the Spirit was given through the laying on of the apostles’ hands, he offered them money,
NRSVCE.19: saying, “Give me also this power so that anyone on whom I lay my hands may receive the Holy Spirit.”
NRSVCE.20: But Peter said to him, “May your silver perish with you, because you thought you could obtain God’s gift with money!
NRSVCE.21: You have no part or share in this, for your heart is not right before God.
NRSVCE.22: Repent therefore of this wickedness of yours, and pray to the Lord that, if possible, the intent of your heart may be forgiven you.
NRSVCE.23: For I see that you are in the gall of bitterness and the chains of wickedness.”
NRSVCE.24: Simon answered, “Pray for me to the Lord, that nothing of what you have said may happen to me.”
NRSVCE.25: Now after Peter and John had testified and spoken the word of the Lord, they returned to Jerusalem, proclaiming the good news to many villages of the Samaritans.
NRSVCE.26: Then an angel of the Lord said to Philip, “Get up and go toward the south to the road that goes down from Jerusalem to Gaza.” (This is a wilderness road.)
NRSVCE.27: So he got up and went. Now there was an Ethiopian eunuch, a court official of the Candace, queen of the Ethiopians, in charge of her entire treasury. He had come to Jerusalem to worship
NRSVCE.28: and was returning home; seated in his chariot, he was reading the prophet Isaiah.
NRSVCE.29: Then the Spirit said to Philip, “Go over to this chariot and join it.”
NRSVCE.30: So Philip ran up to it and heard him reading the prophet Isaiah. He asked, “Do you understand what you are reading?”
NRSVCE.31: He replied, “How can I, unless someone guides me?” And he invited Philip to get in and sit beside him.
NRSVCE.32:
Now the passage of the scripture that he was reading was this: “Like a sheep he was led to the slaughter,
and like a lamb silent before its shearer,
so he does not open his mouth.
NRSVCE.33:
In his humiliation justice was denied him.
Who can describe his generation?
For his life is taken away from the earth.”
NRSVCE.34: The eunuch asked Philip, “About whom, may I ask you, does the prophet say this, about himself or about someone else?”
NRSVCE.35: Then Philip began to speak, and starting with this scripture, he proclaimed to him the good news about Jesus.
NRSVCE.36: As they were going along the road, they came to some water; and the eunuch said, “Look, here is water! What is to prevent me from being baptized?”
NRSVCE.38: He commanded the chariot to stop, and both of them, Philip and the eunuch, went down into the water, and Philip baptized him.
NRSVCE.39: When they came up out of the water, the Spirit of the Lord snatched Philip away; the eunuch saw him no more, and went on his way rejoicing.
NRSVCE.40: But Philip found himself at Azotus, and as he was passing through the region, he proclaimed the good news to all the towns until he came to Caesarea.

NET.1: And Saul agreed completely with killing him. Now on that day a great persecution began against the church in Jerusalem, and all except the apostles were forced to scatter throughout the regions of Judea and Samaria.
NET.2: Some devout men buried Stephen and made loud lamentation over him.
NET.3: But Saul was trying to destroy the church; entering one house after another, he dragged off both men and women and put them in prison.
NET.4: Now those who had been forced to scatter went around proclaiming the good news of the word.
NET.5: Philip went down to the main city of Samaria and began proclaiming the Christ to them.
NET.6: The crowds were paying attention with one mind to what Philip said, as they heard and saw the miraculous signs he was performing.
NET.7: For unclean spirits, crying with loud shrieks, were coming out of many who were possessed, and many paralyzed and lame people were healed.
NET.8: So there was great joy in that city.
NET.9: Now in that city was a man named Simon, who had been practicing magic and amazing the people of Samaria, claiming to be someone great.
NET.10: All the people, from the least to the greatest, paid close attention to him, saying, “This man is the power of God that is called ‘Great.’”
NET.11: And they paid close attention to him because he had amazed them for a long time with his magic.
NET.12: But when they believed Philip as he was proclaiming the good news about the kingdom of God and the name of Jesus Christ, they began to be baptized, both men and women.
